Update data views that have scripted fields to use runtime fields instead. In most cases, you will only need to change "return <value>;" to "emit(<value>);".
Alternatively, you can achieve similar functionality by computing values at query time using the Elasticsearch Query Language (ES|QL).

dataViews.advancedSettings.cacheMaxAgeTe: Sets how long data view fields API requests are cached in seconds. A value of 0 turns off caching. sharedUXPackages.dateRangePicker.calenda: Calendar, use arrow keys to navigate days. Press Page Up/Down to navigate months.
Select an end date to apply
sharedUXPackages.dateRangePicker.documen: Type `to` to split start and end dates, otherwise the input will consider it relative to now.
Use formats like `Dec 1, 2025, 00:00` (default), `2025-12-01` (ISO 8601), `Fri, 1 Dec 2025 00:00:00 GMT` (RFC 2822), or `1760665383890` (unix timestamp).
Type `-5m`, `past 5 min`, `last 5 minutes` or any combination with words `last`, `next`, `ago`, `from now` etc.
Custom time ranges are combinations of the aforementioned formats, e.g. `now to Fri, 1 Dec 2025 00:00:00 GMT` or `-12d to now`.
sharedUXPackages.dateRangePicker.customT: Start time will be set to the time of the refresh.
End time will be set to the time of the refresh.
You can type this directly in the time picker to get the same time range
End date must be later than start date
